CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-06-2026 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-03-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-06-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgement, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Defendant Carlos Monterrosa
Off Calendar Oct-17-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. Lawrence Murray, Esq. (via Zoom videoconference) of Murray and Associates (tel: 415-673-0555) for the Plaintiffs.
Atty. Nicholas Wood, Esq. of Phillips, Spallas & Angstadt LLP (tel: 415-278-9400) for Defendant Yuri Zuniga Romero.
Atty. Heather Tichenor, Esq. of Open Door Legal (tel: 415-612-3032) for Defendant Carlos Monterrosa.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CARLOS MONTERROSA's Motion For Summary Judgement, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Defendant Carlos Monterrosa.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court vacates its tentative ruling, and denies the motion as to causes of action 1 through 3.

Counsel Lawrence Murray is to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Court reporter: Katie Wiggins, CSR #13961 (tel: 916-475-5878 / email: kwiggins0616@gmail.com) =(301/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. Lawrence Murray, Esq. (via Zoom videoconference) of Murray and Associates (tel: 415-673-0555) for the Plaintiffs.
Atty. Nicholas Wood, Esq. of Phillips, Spallas & Angstadt LLP (tel: 415-278-9400) for Defendant Yuri Zuniga Romero.
Atty. Heather Tichenor, Esq. of Open Door Legal (tel: 415-612-3032) for Defendant Carlos Monterrosa.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT YURI ZUNIGA ROMERO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court vacates its tentative ruling. The Court grants summary adjudication as to the second cause of action, and denies summary adjudication as to the third cause of action.

Counsel Lawrence Murray is to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Court reporter: Katie Wiggins, CSR #13961 (tel: 916-475-5878 / email: kwiggins0616@gmail.com) =(301/HEK)

Motion For Order Permitting Filing Of An Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-10-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MIGUEL GARCIA's Motion For Order Permitting Filing Of An Amended Complaint

No appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF MIGUEL GARCIA'S Motion For Order Permitting Filing Of An Amended Complaint, as follows:

Plaintiff's "motion for order permitting filing of an amended complaint" is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP 428.50(c).) A proposed amended complaint's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

One order signed for the 2 motions.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)
